specification.scope_notes: Acme Corp as a standalone entity, excluding subsidiaries.
specification.target: Acme Corp's market position and revenue trajectory over 12 months

resolution_criteria.threshold: Revenue growth decelerates to below 10% YoY by Q1 2027
Market share declines by 2+ percentage points
Two or more C-suite departures within the horizon

baseline.position: Consensus expects 15-20% revenue growth and stable market position
baseline.divergence: Seer projects deceleration driven by R-channel bottleneck in enterprise partnerships
baseline.hinge: Whether enterprise partnership pipeline converts or stalls under relational trust deficit

key_findings: Revenue grew 22% YoY in FY2025 but decelerated from 30% in FY2024
Enterprise partnership pipeline shows 40% lower conversion rate than prior year
Two key enterprise accounts paused renewal negotiations citing trust concerns
Structural channel (S) strong: product architecture rated best-in-class by analysts
Relational channel (R) under stress: Glassdoor scores declining, partner NPS down 15 points
Foundational channel (F) adequate: 18 months runway at current burn
critical_gaps: No direct access to internal partnership pipeline data — relying on public signals and proxy evidence
Employee sentiment data is proxy for R-channel health — actual internal trust dynamics unobserved

proxy_evidence.description: Glassdoor scores and partner NPS as proxy for R-channel health
proxy_evidence.transfer_conditions: Transfers when external sentiment correlates with internal relational dynamics. Breaks if public-facing metrics are managed independently of actual trust conditions.

contradictions: Strong S-channel (product) and declining R-channel (relationships) — product excellence may mask relational erosion for 1-2 quarters before pipeline effects become visible in revenue

go_no_go.rationale: Evidence is adequate for structural diagnosis but R-channel assessment relies heavily on proxy evidence. Confidence bands should reflect this dependency.

forecast.statement: Acme Corp's revenue growth will decelerate to below 15% YoY by Q4 2026, driven by R-channel bottleneck (enterprise partnership trust deficit) that S-channel product strength cannot compensate for beyond Q2 2026. Market share erosion of 1-3 percentage points likely by end of horizon.

scenarios.label: Base case: R-channel drag
scenarios.description: Enterprise partnership conversion continues declining. Revenue growth drops to 10-14% by Q4 2026. Market share erodes 1-2 points. No C-suite departures but increasing internal tension.
scenarios.structural_driver: R-channel bottleneck deepens. Strong S-channel buys time through Q2 but cannot compensate once pipeline effects hit revenue.

scenarios.label: Accelerated decline
scenarios.description: Key enterprise partner defections trigger cascade. Revenue growth drops below 10%. Market share erodes 2-4 points. At least one C-suite departure.
scenarios.structural_driver: R-channel failure cascades to I-channel (loss of market signal from partner network) and F-channel (revenue shortfall reduces runway).

scenarios.label: Stabilization
scenarios.description: Leadership recognizes R-channel bottleneck and redirects resources. Revenue growth stabilizes at 15-18%. Market share holds. Trust metrics begin recovering by Q1 2027.
scenarios.structural_driver: Navigator correction (lambda recovery). System shifts from resistance (D_r) to preparation (D_p). R-channel investment begins.

scenarios.label: Upside surprise
scenarios.description: New product launch or acquisition bypasses partnership channel entirely. Revenue growth exceeds 20%. Market share gains.
scenarios.structural_driver: Exogenous boundary encounter changes the field. Low probability because structural bottleneck persists regardless of product events.

confidence_provenance.evidence_strength: Adequate. Financial data is primary; R-channel evidence is mostly proxy. Pushes confidence down.
confidence_provenance.structural_clarity: Clear. R-channel bottleneck is well-supported by multiple converging signals. Pushes confidence up.
confidence_provenance.proxy_dependence: Moderate. R-channel assessment relies on Glassdoor and partner NPS as proxies. Widens uncertainty.
confidence_provenance.shock_sensitivity: Moderate. A major product launch or acquisition could bypass the bottleneck. Scenario 4 captures this.
confidence_provenance.challenge_impact: The S-R compensation question is the main uncertainty. How long can strong product mask weak relationships? Evidence suggests 1-2 quarters, but this is estimated, not measured.

falsification_criteria: Revenue growth accelerates above 20% YoY in any quarter during the horizon (contradicts deceleration thesis)
Enterprise partnership conversion rate recovers to FY2024 levels without structural R-channel intervention (contradicts bottleneck diagnosis)
Market share increases by 1+ percentage points (contradicts erosion prediction)
monitoring_triggers: Quarterly earnings release — check revenue growth rate and guidance
Major enterprise partnership announcement (positive or negative)
Glassdoor score movement of >0.3 points in either direction

baseline_contrast.consensus: Wall Street consensus expects 15-20% revenue growth and stable market position, driven by product strength.
baseline_contrast.divergence: Seer projects deceleration that consensus misses because consensus weights product (S-channel) heavily and underweights relational dynamics (R-channel).
baseline_contrast.hinge: If enterprise partnership pipeline converts at FY2024 rates, consensus is right and Seer is wrong. If conversion continues declining, Seer's structural read is vindicated.

diagnostic_summary.regime: Resistance (D_r) — defending current growth narrative despite relational deterioration
diagnostic_summary.margin_posture: dM/dt slightly negative. 18 months runway provides buffer, but margin is being consumed by R-channel compensation.
diagnostic_summary.field_fit: chi_S high (product-market fit strong), chi_R declining (partnership ecosystem trust eroding), chi_F adequate
diagnostic_summary.navigation_profile: Visionary quadrant — high m (ambitious product roadmap), low lambda (slow to incorporate relational signals into strategy)

outcome_evidence: Acme Corp FY2026 revenue growth: 12% YoY (below consensus 15-20%)
Market share declined 1.8 percentage points in core segment
No C-suite departures during the horizon
Enterprise partnership conversion rate remained at FY2025 levels (did not recover to FY2024)

component_scores.outcome: Revenue growth decelerated to 12% YoY — within Seer's base case range of 10-14%. Below consensus.
Market share declined 1.8 points — within Seer's predicted 1-3 point range.
No C-suite departures. Seer's base case predicted no departures but flagged possibility. Partially confirmed.

resolution.baseline_outcome: Wall Street consensus predicted 15-20% growth and stable market position. Actual: 12% growth and 1.8 point market share decline. Consensus was substantially wrong on both dimensions.
resolution.calibration_notes: Seer's R-channel bottleneck diagnosis was vindicated — enterprise partnership conversion did not recover and revenue deceleration followed. The hinge (partnership pipeline) resolved in Seer's direction. Confidence band of 55-65% was well-calibrated given the outcome. Main miss: leadership stability prediction was soft — the criterion was less diagnostic than revenue and market share.
